title: Wellington Declared a State of Emergency as 11-Meter Waves Hit the Coast
script: Waves swamped a capital city? On June 8, New Zealand's capital Wellington declared a state of emergency after forecasts showed waves up to 10 meters — 33 feet — slamming the south coast. Hundreds of residents in Owhiro Bay, Island Bay, Houghton Bay, and Breaker Bay were told to evacuate immediately. Wind gusts hit 128 kilometers per hour at Wellington Airport, canceling dozens of flights. The largest recorded wave peaked at 10.9 meters — nearly 36 feet — off the Wairarapa coast. Coastlines flooded. Roads closed. A Civil Defence controller said the swells were far higher than anything Wellington had seen in years. By June 9, the state of emergency was lifted and residents began returning home. Was Wellington's south coast built to handle swells like this?
